recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Cauliflower Cheese Soup With Buttered Sourdough Croutons

Creamy cauliflower cheese soup blended with cheddar, hard cheese and milk, finished with olive oil, black pepper and dried thyme.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 40 minutes

Yield: 3 soup bowls

Meal role: cheap family dinner

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Cowbelle Whole Milk 3.6% Fat - 500ml
- Emporium British Mature Cheddar Cheese - 150g
- Emporium Grated Hard Cheese - 80g
- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 20g

### Fruit/Veg

- Nature's Pick British Cauliflower - 1 whole cauliflower
- Nature's Pick Brown Onions - 1 medium onion
- Nature's Pick Garlic - 3 small cloves

### Pantry

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 2 slices, about 120g total
- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 1 3/4 tbsp (26.2ml)
- Ready, Set... Cook! Dried Thyme - 1 1/2 tsp (1.5g)
- Morrisons Dijon Mustard - 5g
- Solesta White Wine Vinegar - 5ml

## Equipment

- 32 inch tri-ply saute pan with lid
- 10 inch cast iron frying pan
- Nutribullet Smart Touch Blender
- Ladle
- Chopping board
- Knife
- Grater
- Spatula or wooden spoon

## Mise En Place

- Onion and garlic bowl: 1 medium onion chopped with 3 small garlic cloves sliced. Used in Step 1.
- Cauliflower bowl: 1 whole cauliflower cut into florets and small stalk pieces, with 1 tsp (1g) dried thyme. Used in Step 2.
- Cheese bowl: 150g grated cheddar, 80g hard cheese, 5g Dijon and 5ml white wine vinegar. Used in Step 4.
- Crouton bowl: 2 slices white sourdough diced with 20g butter ready by the cast iron pan. Used in Step 3.
- Serving setup: 3 soup bowls, 1 tbsp (15ml) olive oil, 1/2 tsp (0.5g) dried thyme and the black pepper grinder. Used in Step 6.

## Method

### 1. Soften the onion and garlic

Heat the olive oil in the saute pan over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic with a pinch of salt. Cook for about 6-7 minutes until soft but not browned.

Uses:

- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 3/4 tbsp (11.2ml)
- Onion and garlic bowl - all

### 2. Simmer the cauliflower

Add the cauliflower, milk, water and dried thyme. Bring to a gentle simmer, cover loosely and cook for about 18-20 minutes until the cauliflower stalk pieces are soft.

Uses:

- Cauliflower bowl - all
- Cowbelle Whole Milk 3.6% Fat - 500ml
- Water - enough to help the liquid cover the cauliflower, about 200ml

### 3. Make the buttered croutons

While the soup simmers, melt the butter in the cast iron pan. Add the diced sourdough and cook for about 5-6 minutes, tossing until golden and crisp.

Uses:

- Crouton bowl - all: 2 diced sourdough slices and 20g butter

### 4. Add cheese and blend smooth

Take the soup off the heat. Stir in the cheese bowl while the soup is still hot so the cheese softens into the cauliflower base. Blend smooth in the Nutribullet jug for about 1-2 minutes, then return the soup to the saute pan.

Uses:

- Simmered cauliflower, milk, onion and garlic soup base - all
- Cheese bowl - all

### 5. Warm and season the soup

Warm the blended soup gently on low heat for about 2-3 minutes until glossy and hot enough to serve. Do not boil hard after the dairy goes in, because hard boiling can make cheese split or turn grainy. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.

Uses:

- Blended cauliflower cheese soup - all

### 6. Serve the dinner bowls

Ladle the finished soup into 3 bowls. Add croutons, then finish each bowl with an olive oil circle, black pepper and dried thyme.

Uses:

- Cauliflower cheese soup - all
- Buttered sourdough croutons - all
- Serving setup - 3 dinner bowls, 1 tbsp (15ml) olive oil, 1/2 tsp (0.5g) dried thyme and black pepper

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Cauliflower Cheese Soup | 45% of the finished soup batch | 33% of the finished soup batch | 22% of the finished soup batch |
| Buttered Sourdough Croutons | 40% of the croutons | 35% of the croutons | 25% of the croutons |
| Olive Oil Thyme Finish | 45% of the olive oil and thyme finish | 33% of the olive oil and thyme finish | 22% of the olive oil and thyme finish |

## Nutrition And Cost

| Person | Kcal | Protein | Carbs | Fat | Cost | Fullness |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Luke | 941.3 | 49.6g | 50.6g | 61.6g | GBP 2.09 | 120.2 |
| Sarah | 714.6 | 37.0g | 40.0g | 46.1g | GBP 1.57 | 91.1 |
| Louis | 483.4 | 24.8g | 27.7g | 31.1g | GBP 1.07 | 61.6 |

## Cook's Notes

- The cheeses go into the hot soup before blending. This matches Luke's proven method and gives the smoothest texture.
- Blend the source-style soup volume once, then return it to the saute pan to warm gently.
- Keep the heat gentle after blending so the dairy stays smooth.
- Salt, black pepper and water are method helpers, so they are not counted in the shopping or nutrition rows.

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Chicken Fajita Plates With Mild Lime Yoghurt Crema

Lime-led marinated chicken fajita plates with charred peppers, Aldi protein wraps, coriander and a mild savoury yoghurt crema.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 12 minutes prep-ahead, 25 minutes cook/serve

Prep-ahead: best done at any convenient point the day before cooking, with at least 1 hour marinating if same-day is needed

Yield: 3 fajita plates, 6 wraps

Meal role: dinner

## Ingredients

### Meat

- Ashfields Chicken Drumstick Fillets - 600g, sliced into thin strips

### Pantry

- Village Bakery Tortilla Protein Wraps - 6 wraps
- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 22.5ml, 1 1/2 tbsp
- Stonemill Smoked Paprika - 5g, 2 1/2 tsp
- Stonemill Ground Cumin - 5g, 2 1/2 tsp
- Stonemill Dried Oregano - 1g, 1 tsp

### Fruit And Veg

- Mixed Peppers - 2 peppers, 320g, sliced from the normal 3-pack
- Brown Onion - 1 medium onion, 110g, sliced
- Garlic - 3 small cloves, 9g, finely grated or chopped
- Limes - 1 1/2 total: 1 lime for the chicken marinade, 1/2 lime fresh for the crema
- Fresh Coriander - 11g, chopped

### Dairy

- Fat Free Greek Style Yoghurt - 160g

## Equipment

- 32 inch tri-ply saute pan with lid
- Mixing bowl
- Small bowl
- Tongs
- Chopping board and knife
- Plates

## Mise En Place

- Chicken marinade bowl: 600g sliced chicken mixed with 1 lime, 22.5ml olive oil, 3 small garlic cloves, 5g smoked paprika, 5g cumin, 1g oregano, salt and black pepper. Used in P1 and Step 2.
- Crema bowl: 160g fat-free Greek yoghurt with the remaining 1/2 lime kept whole until cook day. Used in Step 1.
- Vegetable board: 2 sliced peppers and 1 sliced medium onion, kept together because they char together. Used in Step 3.
- Wrap station: 6 protein wraps ready by the pan. Used in Step 4 and Step 5.
- Finish station: 11g chopped coriander ready for plating. Used in Step 5.

## Method

### Prep Ahead

#### P1. Marinate the chicken

Mix the chicken marinade with 1 lime, oil, garlic and spices. Add the chicken, mix well, cover and chill. Keep the remaining 1/2 lime whole for the crema on cook day. Best result is day-before prep when convenient, with at least 1 hour if same-day is needed.

Uses:

- Chicken marinade bowl - all: 600g chicken, 1 lime, 22.5ml olive oil, 3 small garlic cloves, 5g smoked paprika, 5g cumin and 1g oregano

### Cook & Serve

#### 1. Make the mild crema

Squeeze the remaining 1/2 lime into the yoghurt and stir until it is tangy, mild and spoonable.

Uses:

- Crema bowl - all: 160g fat-free Greek yoghurt and 1/2 lime, juiced fresh

#### 2. Char the chicken

Heat the 32 inch tri-ply saute pan until properly hot. Cook the chicken in one batch for about 7-8 minutes, leaving it alone at first so it chars before turning. Lift the cooked chicken out to a plate.

Uses:

- Chicken marinade bowl - all marinated chicken

#### 3. Char the peppers and onion

Use the same hot pan for the peppers and onion. Cook for about 5-6 minutes, until they pick up char but still have bite.

Uses:

- Vegetable board - all sliced peppers and onion

#### 4. Combine the fajita mix and warm the wraps

Return the chicken to the pan with the vegetables for 1-2 minutes. Warm the protein wraps one at a time in the dry pan, about 30-45 seconds each.

Uses:

- Chicken and vegetables - all
- Wrap station - 6 protein wraps

#### 5. Plate

Serve this as fajita plates, not one overloaded wrap. Put the finished fajita mix on the plate, add the crema, coriander, and serve the warm wraps alongside each plate.

Uses:

- Mild lime yoghurt crema - all
- Finish station - 11g chopped coriander

## Plate

| Finished element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Finished fajita mix | about half, served with 3 wraps | about one third, served with 2 wraps | about one sixth, served with 1 wrap |
| Warm protein wraps | 3 wraps | 2 wraps | 1 wrap |
| Mild lime yoghurt crema | 75g | 60g | 25g |
| Coriander finish | 5g coriander | 4g coriander | 2g coriander |

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 1080.8 kcal, 100.1g protein, 106.7g carbs, 28.5g fat, GBP 3.37, 145.3 fullness
- Sarah: 726.1 kcal, 67.1g protein, 72.1g carbs, 18.7g fat, GBP 2.28, 98.7 fullness
- Louis: 361.2 kcal, 33.3g protein, 35.7g carbs, 9.4g fat, GBP 1.14, 48.2 fullness

## Cook's Notes

- The marinating wait is a prep-ahead task, not active cooking time. If this is dinner on Wednesday, prep the chicken at any convenient point on Tuesday. Do not treat this as an exact 24-hour timer.
- The chicken is one hot batch in the 32 inch saute pan. Keep the pan hot and leave the chicken alone at first so it chars rather than steams.
- Keep the heat high enough for char, then warm the wraps after the fajita mix is ready.
- The wraps are carriers for the plate. They are not expected to close around the whole dinner portion.
- The yoghurt crema is a mild savoury sauce, not a sweet yoghurt topping.
- This is a large dinner against the normal target range, but it is inside the repaired hard dinner limits and should be balanced by the rest of the day.

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Buttered Poached Eggs On White Sourdough Toast

Soft poached eggs on hot buttered white sourdough, seasoned simply at the plate.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 25 minutes

Yield: 3 plates

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs - 8 eggs
- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 15g total

### Pantry

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 3 slices, about 180g total

## Equipment

- 20 cm saucepan with lid
- Slotted spoon
- Toaster
- Plates

## Mise En Place

- Toast board: 3 slices of white sourdough with 15g unsalted butter ready beside them. Used in Step 2.
- Egg cups: 8 eggs, cracked into cups if you want cleaner poaching, or kept whole if cracking straight into the water. Used in Step 3.
- Finish station: salt and black pepper beside the plates. Used in Step 4.

## Method

### 1. Heat the poaching water

Bring the saucepan of water to a gentle simmer, then lower the heat so the water is moving but not bubbling hard. This usually takes about 5 minutes.

Uses:

- Water - enough for poaching

### 2. Toast and butter the sourdough

Toast the sourdough until golden and firm. Butter each slice while it is hot so the butter melts into the toast. This should take about 4 minutes.

Uses:

- Toast board - all: 3 slices white sourdough and 15g unsalted butter

### 3. Poach the eggs

Slide the eggs into the water in controlled rounds if the pan is crowded. Cook gently until the whites are set and the yolks are still soft. Allow about 3-4 minutes per round.

Uses:

- Egg cups - all: 8 eggs

### 4. Plate and season

Lift the eggs out with a slotted spoon and drain briefly. Plate using the table below, then finish each plate with salt and black pepper.

Uses:

- Buttered sourdough toast - all
- Poached eggs - all
- Finish station - salt and black pepper to taste

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Buttery Scrambled Eggs On White Sourdough Toast

Soft scrambled eggs cooked slowly in butter and spooned over hot buttered white sourdough.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 18 minutes

Yield: 3 plates

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs - 8 eggs
- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 23g total

### Pantry

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 3 slices, about 180g total

## Equipment

- 32 inch tri-ply saute pan with lid
- Jug
- Fork or small whisk
- Silicone spatula
- Toaster
- Plates

## Mise En Place

- Toast board: 3 slices of white sourdough with 15g butter ready beside them. Used in Step 3.
- Egg jug: 8 eggs cracked into a jug with a light pinch of salt and a light grind of black pepper. Used in Step 2 and Step 4.
- Pan butter dish: 8g butter ready by the saute pan. Used in Step 4.

## Method

### 1. Warm the saute pan

Put the saute pan on the lowest heat for about 2-3 minutes so the base warms gently.

Uses:

- Saute pan - low heat

### 2. Whisk the eggs

Whisk the eggs in the jug for about 1 minute until the yolks and whites are mostly combined.

Uses:

- Egg jug - all: 8 eggs

### 3. Toast and butter the sourdough

Put the sourdough in the toaster. When the slices are done, butter each one while hot. This should take about 4 minutes.

Uses:

- Toast board - all: 3 slices white sourdough and 15g butter

### 4. Cook the eggs softly

Melt 8g butter in the warm pan. Pour in the eggs, then slowly scrape the bottom up with the spatula for about 4-5 minutes until the eggs are almost cooked but still soft.

Uses:

- Egg jug - all whisked eggs
- Pan butter dish - 8g butter

### 5. Finish off the heat

Take the pan off the heat while the eggs are still slightly glossy. Spoon the scrambled eggs over the buttered slices using the table below.

Uses:

- Buttered sourdough toast - all
- Scrambled eggs - all

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Buttered sourdough toast | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice |
| Soft scrambled eggs | about two fifths of the egg batch, 3 eggs source-equivalent | about two fifths of the egg batch, 3 eggs source-equivalent | about one quarter of the egg batch, 2 eggs source-equivalent |

## Nutrition And Cost

| Person | Kcal | Protein | Carbs | Fat | Cost | Fullness |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Luke | 399.5 | 24.4g | 28.3g | 21.8g | GBP 0.88 | 95.1 |
| Sarah | 399.5 | 24.4g | 28.3g | 21.8g | GBP 0.88 | 95.1 |
| Louis | 325.0 | 17.9g | 28.3g | 16.4g | GBP 0.69 | 74.6 |

## Cook's Notes

- Keep the heat low. The eggs should cook gently rather than sizzle.
- Scrape the bottom of the pan slowly so the eggs form soft curds.
- Take the pan off the heat before the eggs look fully cooked; the carry-over heat finishes them.
- The butter total is measured as 15g for the toast and 8g for the pan.
- Salt and black pepper are neutral helpers, so they are mentioned in the method but not counted in the shopping or nutrition rows.

# Strawberry Banana Fat-Free Greek Yoghurt Bowl With Honey And Chia

Fresh strawberry and banana bowls with fat-free Greek-style yoghurt, measured honey and chia.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 8 minutes

Yield: 3 bowls

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Brooklea Fat Free Greek Style Yogurt - 730g total

### Fruit/Veg

- Nature's Pick Bananas - 2 bananas
- Nature's Pick Strawberries - 140g

### Pantry

- Everyday Essentials Clear Honey - 15g total
- Chia seeds - 15g total

## Equipment

- 3 breakfast bowls
- Chopping board
- Knife
- Scales
- Spoon

## Mise En Place

- Fruit board: 2 bananas sliced and 140g strawberries washed, hulled and sliced. Used in Step 1 and Step 3.
- Bowl set: 3 breakfast bowls on the scales with the yoghurt tub and spoon ready. Used in Step 2.
- Finish station: honey and chia seeds ready beside the bowls. Used in Step 4.

## Method

### 1. Prep the fruit

Wash and hull the strawberries, then peel and slice 2 bananas. This should take about 3 minutes.

Uses:

- Fruit board - all: 2 bananas and 140g strawberries

### 2. Spoon in the yoghurt

Spoon the yoghurt straight into the bowls.

Uses:

- Bowl set - yoghurt: Luke 290g, Sarah 260g, Louis 180g

### 3. Add the fruit

Share the sliced banana by weight, then add strawberries on top.

Uses:

- Fruit board - banana slices: Luke 85g, Sarah 60g, Louis 55g
- Fruit board - strawberries: Luke 50g, Sarah 50g, Louis 40g

### 4. Finish with honey and chia

Add honey and chia to each bowl. Serve straight away.

Uses:

- Finish station - 5g honey per bowl
- Finish station - 5g chia seeds per bowl

## Plate

| Element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Greek yoghurt base | 290g | 260g | 180g |
| Fresh fruit | 85g banana + 50g strawberries | 60g banana + 50g strawberries | 55g banana + 40g strawberries |
| Honey and chia finish | 5g honey + 5g chia | 5g honey + 5g chia | 5g honey + 5g chia |

## Nutrition And Cost

| Person | Kcal | Protein | Carbs | Fat | Cost | Fullness |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Luke | 302.4 | 19.2g | 52.7g | 2.5g | GBP 0.95 | 108.3 |
| Sarah | 263.5 | 17.1g | 44.9g | 2.4g | GBP 0.88 | 96.6 |
| Louis | 206.9 | 12.3g | 35.9g | 2.2g | GBP 0.68 | 71.6 |

## Cook's Notes

- Use 2 bananas total, sliced once, then weigh the banana into the bowls. This avoids a whole banana each and avoids leftover fruit.
- Louis eats the same as the adults here. Only spice or strong heat should be removed for him by default.
- Serve straight away so the banana stays fresh and the chia does not thicken the bowls too much.
- This is a lighter adult breakfast. The day planner must balance the adult protein gap later in the day.
